body_txt: Swords are awesome. For a hero worth their salt, they are practically a requirement , sometimes the bigger the better . But there's one tiny little flaw: a complete lack of range . Now, how would your One-Man Army hero be able to routinely face and prevail over entire groups of enemies packing (cross) bows, firearms or ranged magic? Well, there are many ways (carrying a gun , bow , or just knowing magic ; using a sword that can become longer , or just throwing it ) depending on your setting. But there's hardly anything that is a more distilled Rule of Cool than Sword Beams . A character will make a slashing motion with their sword, but at a distance, optionally charging it with power right before this . What springs forth is a beam of Pure Energy , traveling through the air or on the ground. Often features Absurd Cutting Power , allowing The Hero to fell entire companies of Mooks in one fell swoop . Arbitrary Weapon Range prevents it from cutting anything outside of the intended battlefield. Another version involves the character stabbing or smashing their sword into the ground and creating a Ground Wave that may or may not actually tear through the earth as it crashes towards the enemy. Projectiles fired by thrusting the sword's end at its target (or even just pointing it in their direction and using it as a Magic Wand ) are rarer, but they do exist. It's worth noting that this trope can also come in different flavors , not just related to their execution. Any weapon that has a blade or point can fit the bill, such as spears or axes; it's not exclusive to swords. Likewise, though the most common form of the Sword Beam is crescent-shaped, it can also take the shape of a pointed star, a literal beam , or even the weapon's shape itself . The resulting projectile can either keep the original size and shape, or cover a full sector of the swing, thus growing larger with distance. When magical, this is a type of Full-Contact Magic (via either Spell Blade or Ki Manipulation ), and when not, it falls under Implausible Fencing Powers . If the projectile is a shockwave of compressed air which cuts things, then it's also a Razor Wind . If used with a Laser Blade , it often looks more like the blade is extending to strike the target . See also Bayonet Ya (for guns with what are basically swords attached) and Mix-and-Match Weapon (for weapons that are also guns), and Throwing Your Sword Always Works (for another, less magical way of using weapons as just ranged weapons). Compare Energy Bow (for bows that shoot beams) and Boom Stick (for the Magic Staff , which is usually a magical tool first, and a weapon second ). Can be combined with Blade Spam for a unique variant of Beam Spam . Not to be confused with Sword Lines , though a Sword Beam is arguably a weaponized version of that effect. For a bladed weapon made of beams, see Laser Blade . For a beam shaped like a weapon, see Shaping Your Attacks .
